alerts.title: Iran president rejects missile program inclusion in US accord amid breakout window
alerts.summary: Iran's president explicitly stated the country's ballistic missile program will never be included in a US Memorandum of Understanding, according to The Jerusalem Post report (incidents feed). This hardening of Iran's negotiating position coincides with continued 60% U-235 enrichment and 1-2 week nuclear breakout estimate (global_metrics), signaling Iran is rejecting key US negotiation preconditions even as talks resume in Switzerland.

alerts.title: Iran war day 118: IRGC rejects alternative Hormuz route amid joint Iran-Oman control plan
alerts.summary: Al Jazeera reports IRGC rejected a new proposed Hormuz shipping route while US Secretary of State Rubio prepares GCC meetings (incidents). Development follows the Iran-Oman joint administration announcement (telegram, 54.6K views) establishing maritime fee collection, indicating Iran is consolidating strategic control over the chokepoint and blocking US-backed workarounds to maintain shipping flow.

alerts.title: Israeli strikes in Lebanon kill 3 as ceasefire framework fractures despite Washington talks
alerts.summary: NBC News and Al Jazeera confirm Israeli strikes killed 2-3 in Beirut suburbs and southern Lebanon, including sea turtle conservationist Mona Khalil (CBC), with Hezbollah asserting ceasefire violation (incidents). Operations continue despite ongoing Washington negotiations and Senate constraints, indicating Israeli unilateral escalation outside formal diplomatic framework.

alerts.title: Iran-linked cyber group claims FBI drone system breach, threatens World Cup targets
alerts.summary: France 24 and Middle East Eye report Iran-linked cyber group claims successful penetration of FBI drone systems and threatens World Cup event targets as retaliation for Iran strikes (global_metrics cyber headlines). Claim reflects escalating Iranian cyber warfare capability and willingness to target critical US infrastructure and international sporting events.

alerts.title: US threatens fresh Iran attacks as talks resume in Switzerland amid ceasefire pause
alerts.summary: Fortune reports Trump administration threatened new attacks on Iran, causing negotiations to pause, even as US-Iran talks are scheduled to resume Sunday in Switzerland with Tehran claiming Strait of Hormuz closure (incidents). Pattern reflects extreme volatility in diplomatic engagement, with US threats destabilizing negotiation window and signaling potential resumption of hostilities.

analysis_note: Iran is hardening its nuclear and strategic positions while consolidating Hormuz control and rejecting US missile program demands, even as ceasefire frameworks fracture and cyber escalation accelerates, creating a critical convergence of nuclear threshold, regional proxy activation, and diplomatic breakdown.
